feature_findings.resolution: Implemented POST /api/public/v1/query, disabled by default and ahead of the legacy production gate.

feature_findings.finding: Browser could supply tenant or service identity.
feature_findings.resolution: Closed request excludes tenant/credential; Authorization and Cookie are rejected; tenant/jurisdiction are server-bound.

feature_findings.finding: Public retrieval could expose foreign/private evidence.
feature_findings.resolution: Forced-RLS tenant transaction and strict public/active/processed/accepted/clean eligibility passed PostgreSQL gates.

feature_findings.finding: Anonymous abuse could bypass retrieval controls.
feature_findings.resolution: Global and HMAC per-client database buckets run before body parsing/retrieval; edge controls remain required.

feature_findings.finding: Comparative evidence could be presented as official answer.
feature_findings.resolution: Comparative/validation-required candidates remain citations or limitations and never produce supported answer content.

feature_findings.finding: Audit could retain query or network identity.
feature_findings.resolution: Audit allowlist excludes query, excerpts, URLs, IP and user-agent; rate identity persists only a 32-byte HMAC digest.

feature_findings.finding: Source links could leak credentials or signatures.
feature_findings.resolution: Public citations require HTTPS URLs with no userinfo, query or fragment.

feature_findings.finding: Feature implementation could be mistaken for deployment.
feature_findings.resolution: Gateway remains disabled by default; no Pages API URL, cloud resource, merge or deployment is claimed.

open_findings.finding: Gateway is implemented but cannot be enabled until an authorized ingested public corpus, edge controls, staging and deployment approval exist.
No authorized durable corpus bytes or real ingestion exist.
No runner has executed the staging lifecycle and twenty API/system journeys.
No approved IdP/BFF/session or authenticated role-aware UI exists.
OS Electoral and Content Agency have not executed consumer suites.
GCP is architecture only; infrastructure, observability, load/HA, recovery, privacy, merge and deployment are absent.
